title: pyrus perpusilla
intro: Pyrus perpusilla, commonly known as the Korean mountain ash, is a species of deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing due to its health benefits. The therapeutic actions of Pyrus perpusilla include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
parts: Pyrus perpusilla has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, bark, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Pyrus perpusilla are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of the plant contain essential oils and glycosides, which are known for their antibacterial and antiviral properties. The bark and roots of Pyrus perpusilla contain tannins and saponins, which are used to treat digestive issues and fever.
side_effects: Pyrus perpusilla can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high concentration of glycosides. Prolonged consumption may lead to liver and kidney damage, resulting from its toxic properties. Additionally, excessive use can cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory problems in sensitive individuals. If not handled correctly, pyrus perpusilla can also interact with other medications, leading to adverse reactions and increased risk of complications.
